channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Multiple broken playground equipment pieces right before school holidays, 18th July
item.description:
​I am writing to follow up on a report made 7 days ago regarding the poor state of this playground. The entire area is outdated, and multiple favorite play structures are completely out of order and blocked off with safety netting. ​With the school summer holidays starting in just a few days, many children are coming here only to leave devastated and crying because they cannot use their favourite equipment. ​In addition to the previously reported issues, I have noticed that the playground talk tubes (the speaking/communication devices) are also completely broken—nothing can be heard through them at all. ​Please look into this urgently. The children need a safe and functioning space to play for the upcoming holidays. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Way, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on bus shelter, 18th July
item.description:
Graffiti on bus shelter. Blue tag. Concern that further graffiti will be seen if this isn’t removed quickly.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Graffiti on lamp post, 18th July
item.description:
Black pen tag on this silver lamp post near Kent House Station, asset id: K 46 4

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Junction of MuswellHill Road and Wood Lane, 18th July
item.description:
Rubbish bags need collecting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Archway Road, Muswell Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hole in road, 18th July
item.description:
Looks like some kind of works has taken place but a large hole been left open in the road and it looks like the repairs have just been abandoned, as this has been here for months now. Needs repairing and put back to normal. We saw a group of kids this morning throwing an old TV in to the hole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Main Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous pavement hazard – urgent repair required, 18th July
item.description:
There are two large holes in the pavement beside a square manhole cover. They create a serious trip hazard and need urgent attention. My child's foot became trapped in one of the holes while walking, and she could easily have broken her leg. Thankfully she wasn't seriously injured, but this highlights how dangerous the defect is, particularly for children, older people, wheelchair users, and anyone pushing a buggy. This pavement is unsafe and poses a significant risk of injury. I would be grateful if this could be inspected and repaired as a matter of urgency before someone is seriously hurt. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Wightman Road, Harringay, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Raised paving edge around utility inspection chamber, 18th July
item.description:
Raised above the ground. Serious trip hazard and health and safety risk

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollybrake Close, Chislehurst, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish dumped in Wyncham Stream, 18th July
item.description:
A large piece of wood has been dumped into the stream on Oaklands. This area of the river is also overgrown and could benefit from a pruning
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Oaklands Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 18th July
item.description:
There is a dead fox on the alley way at the back of the houses of Brook Street 180-178. This is a walk through for a number of people and causing a health hazard.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Collindale Avenue, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish on tree on way to school, 18th July
item.description:
Self explanatory

item.title: The dead fox is on the left hand corner of nightingale Road, 18th July
item.description:
Its hot and there are flies staring to gather by the dead fox 1 not a pleasant sight.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nightingale Road, Poverest, Greater London

item.title: Clearing of brambles, weeds and graffiti, 18th July
item.description:

item.title: Microwave Still On Street, 18th July
item.description:
Have already reported this once and it said it has been fixed and it hasn’t. The microwave is still in the middle of the street.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10, Brunswick Park, Camberwell, Greater London

item.title: 33 Windmill Street W1, 18th July
item.description:
Please could this bicycle be removed? It's been here for many months now. Thank you

item.title: There is a large mass of tree growth from the base which is extending on to the pavement, 18th July
item.description:
This tree is heavily overgrown at the base. It's steadily been getting worse in the past 10 years I've lived here
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Beverley Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: The usual, 18th July
item.description:
Just more lime bikes dumped. The council should start finding these companies for all the flytipped bikes all over the place.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beechmont Close, Downham, Greater London

item.title: Bins in West Heath rec are overflowing, 18th July
item.description:
